articles.title: Oil spikes, markets swoon. Now what? A disciplined approach to the West Asia crisis
articles.description: Geopolitical shocks like the West Asian conflict can unsettle markets, but history shows resilience. For India, managing oil price spikes and inflation is key. Investors should stick to disciplined plans, rebalancing portfolios and upgrading quality during dips. Long-term growth remains the focus, urging calm, liquidity, and discipline.

articles.title: How GIFT City can reshape cross-border investing for domestic and international investors
articles.description: GIFT City is transforming global investing for Indians and attracting foreign capital. Its unique IFSC structure offers a unified regulatory framework. NRIs and foreign investors find investing in India simpler with relaxed compliance and tax norms. The city is rapidly scaling as a financial hub, providing regulatory comfort and a stable environment for capital allocation.

articles.title: Middle East tensions dent housing sales in Q1 2026 in India; demand dips 7% QoQ: ANAROCK
articles.description: Geopolitical tensions have impacted India's housing market, with sales dipping 7% sequentially in Q1 2026, despite a 7% annual rise. New launches outpaced sales, leading to a 4% quarterly increase in unsold inventory. While developers remain confident, global uncertainties and rising costs are affecting buyer sentiment and investment, particularly from Middle Eastern buyers.

articles.title: Concurrent Losers: 13 small-cap stocks decline for 5 consecutive sessions
articles.description: Over the five trading sessions leading up to March 27, the Sensex benchmark fell by 0.84%, shedding 624 points to close at 73,583. During this span (March 19 to March 27), the index declined in two out of the five sessions. Meanwhile, about 17 stocks in the BSE small-cap index registered continuous losses across all five sessions. Of these, 13 stocks recorded cumulative declines of between 10% and 25% over the same period. (Data Source: ACE Equity)"

articles.title: Concurrent Losers: 13 small-cap stocks decline for 5 consecutive sessions
articles.description: Over the five trading sessions leading up to March 27, the Sensex benchmark fell by 0.84%, shedding 624 points to close at 73,583. During this span (March 19 to March 27), the index declined in two out of the five sessions. Meanwhile, about 17 stocks in the BSE small-cap index registered continuous losses across all five sessions. Of these, 13 stocks recorded cumulative declines of between 10% and 25% over the same period. (Data Source: ACE Equity)"

articles.title: Oil spikes, markets swoon. Now what? A disciplined approach to the West Asia crisis
articles.description: Geopolitical shocks like the West Asian conflict can unsettle markets, but history shows resilience. For India, managing oil price spikes and inflation is key. Investors should stick to disciplined plans, rebalancing portfolios and upgrading quality during dips. Long-term growth remains the focus, urging calm, liquidity, and discipline.

articles.title: How GIFT City can reshape cross-border investing for domestic and international investors
articles.description: GIFT City is transforming global investing for Indians and attracting foreign capital. Its unique IFSC structure offers a unified regulatory framework. NRIs and foreign investors find investing in India simpler with relaxed compliance and tax norms. The city is rapidly scaling as a financial hub, providing regulatory comfort and a stable environment for capital allocation.

articles.title: Amit Shah to release BJP's list of charges against TMC on March 28
articles.description: The ‘charge sheet’ will highlight the alleged “failures” of the TMC Government across 14 sectors, the BJP leader said

articles.title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: Constituency Watch – a compilation
articles.description: Tune in to The Hindu’s Constituency Watch segment to follow our on-ground Assembly election coverage

articles.title: Middle East tensions dent housing sales in Q1 2026 in India; demand dips 7% QoQ: ANAROCK
articles.description: Geopolitical tensions have impacted India's housing market, with sales dipping 7% sequentially in Q1 2026, despite a 7% annual rise. New launches outpaced sales, leading to a 4% quarterly increase in unsold inventory. While developers remain confident, global uncertainties and rising costs are affecting buyer sentiment and investment, particularly from Middle Eastern buyers.

dailyNews.description: Joe Rogan and Dave Smith argue Trump's Iran war has shattered his historic 2024 coalition, and Smith warned it could hand the country back to Democrats.

dailyNews.title: At CPAC, the ‘America First’ Crowd Ponders Giving War a Chance
dailyNews.description: The Conservative Political Action Conference has been an America First gathering for years, but with President Trump pursuing war in Iran, organizers have tried to make a shift, with mixed results.

dailyNews.title: Trump Offers More Aid to Farmers, a Key Support Bloc Hurt by Tariffs and War
dailyNews.description: The president announced new loan guarantees at a White House event that appeared intended to reassure a group that has found itself caught in the crosshairs of some of his major policies.

dailyNews.title: Outside Kennedy Center, Jane Fonda and Joan Baez Raise Voices in Protest
dailyNews.description: The pair joined in a gathering of artists and others who denounced censorship and faulted President Trump’s growing influence over the nation’s cultural life.

dailyNews.title: Kennedy Center Performer Asks Judge to Toss Case He Calls ‘Retaliatory’
dailyNews.description: A jazz drummer who called off a performance in protest of the center’s being renamed for President Trump said the president’s allies had sued him as punishment.

dailyNews.title: Iran war rages, Strait of Hormuz still blocked as Trump pushes for a deal
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration is awaiting Iran's formal response to its 15-point peace proposal, as Tehran continues blocking the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" guests for March 29, 2026
dailyNews.description: This week's guests include White House border czar Tom Homan, Democratic Rep. Jim Himes, and former U.S. Surgeon General Dr. Jerome Adams.

dailyNews.title: 2/20: The Takeout with Major Garrett
dailyNews.description: Supreme Court strikes down Trump's emergency tariffs; U.S. and Canada to face off for gold medal in men's hockey

dailyNews.title: Iran claims Strait of Hormuz is closed as Trump says talks are underway
dailyNews.description: According to a statement from Iran's Revolutionary Guards, the Strait of Hormuz is closed, and they vowed a severe response to any ship trying to get through. That seemed to contradict President Trump's claim that Iran had allowed 10 ships through the Strait as a gift. Holly Williams has details.

dailyNews.title: House GOP attempts to forge own path toward ending shutdown, disregarding Senate-backed bill
dailyNews.description: House lawmakers are at odds over a Senate bill that includes TSA pay, but excludes ICE funding. Eleanor Mueller, White House economic policy reporter for Semafor, and Riley Rogerson, House leadership reporter for Politico, join "The Takeout" to discuss.

dailyNews.title: Ambitious, authoritarian and talking to Trump? The hard-liners rising in Iran
dailyNews.description: The Israeli military has systematically killed Iran’s top leaders, so when President Donald Trump said this week that talks are being held with Iran, the biggest question was: Who exactly is the U.S. talking to

dailyNews.title: Markets plunge and U.S. oil hits $100 as Trump's attempt to reassure Wall Street fails
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump is used to bending financial markets to his will
